# BOKASHI

> A local-first Japanese colour instrument for building, inspecting, sampling, and exporting gradients from a source-attributed 250-colour catalogue.

BOKASHI provides nine deterministic visual instruments: Make, Recipes, Mono, Air, Dither, Cube, Image, 3D Plane, and Colours. Rendering and source analysis happen in the browser. No uploaded image or audio leaves the device.

## Primary links

- [Open BOKASHI](https://bokashi.pr0xy.dev/)
- [Source code](https://github.com/xpr0xy/bokashi)
- [Colour data source](https://github.com/xiaohk/nippon-colors)

## Capabilities

- Directly manipulate gradient stops and radial or conic centres on the canvas.
- Browse 120 deterministic recipes and 250 source-attributed colours.
- Extract catalogue-matched palettes from local images and derive colour from local audio analysis.
- Preserve instrument state in a shareable URL.
- Export PNG, JPG, TIFF, SVG, CSS, and JSON.

## Limits

The catalogue is a design reference adapted from xiaohk/nippon-colors under the MIT license. It is not presented as historical scholarship.
